text:
DEPARTMENT OF PATHOLOGY AND LABORATORY MEDICINE
SURGICAL PATHOLOGY REPORT

PATIENT: Raymond Okafor
MRN: [REDACTED]
DATE OF PROCEDURE: [DATE]
DATE OF REPORT: [DATE]
ATTENDING PATHOLOGIST: [REDACTED]
REQUESTING PHYSICIAN: [REDACTED], MD, Medical Oncology
CLINICAL HISTORY: 58-year-old male with known Stage IV sigmoid colon adenocarcinoma (KRAS G12D mutant, MSS) with bilobar hepatic metastases and bilateral pulmonary metastases. Currently on second-line therapy with FOLFIRI plus ramucirumab. Imaging demonstrates ongoing partial response; re-biopsy of dominant hepatic lesion requested for repeat molecular profiling and correlation with liquid biopsy to evaluate for acquired resistance mechanisms. Prior treatments: FOLFOX plus bevacizumab (12 cycles), laparoscopic sigmoid colectomy, maintenance 5-FU/leucovorin plus bevacizumab.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
SPECIMEN IDENTIFICATION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Specimen A: CT-GUIDED CORE NEEDLE BIOPSY, LIVER, SEGMENT VII MASS
  Submitted in formalin, labeled with patient name and MRN.
  Specimen consists of two core needle biopsy fragments submitted in a single formalin-filled container.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
TYPE OF PROCEDURE
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

CT-guided percutaneous core needle biopsy, right hepatic lobe, segment VII lesion (18-gauge needle, 3 passes). Procedure performed by Interventional Radiology. Clinical indication: re-biopsy of known hepatic metastasis for repeat comprehensive genomic profiling and assessment of potential acquired resistance mechanisms in the setting of metastatic colorectal adenocarcinoma on systemic therapy.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
GROSS DESCRIPTION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Specimen A: Received in formalin are two tan-white core needle biopsy fragments. The fragments measure 1.3 cm and 1.1 cm in length, each approximately 1 mm in diameter. The tissue is firm, slightly pale tan, and shows no gross hemorrhage or necrosis. The cores are entirely submitted in one cassette (A1) following routine processing. Gross photograph taken. Representative sections submitted in entirety.

  Cassette summary:
    A1 -- Two core biopsy fragments, entirely submitted (2 fragments)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DIAGNOSTIC FINDINGS
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Specimen A: CT-GUIDED CORE NEEDLE BIOPSY, LIVER, SEGMENT VII MASS

DIAGNOSIS: METASTATIC ADENOCARCINOMA, CONSISTENT WITH COLORECTAL PRIMARY.

Histologic Features:
- The biopsy demonstrates hepatic parenchyma infiltrated by a moderately differentiated (grade 2) adenocarcinoma. The neoplastic glands are arranged in irregular cribriform and tubular configurations, with focal pseudostratification of nuclei. Individual tumor cells are polygonal to columnar, with abundant pale-to-eosinophilic cytoplasm, vesicular to hyperchromatic nuclei, and prominent nucleoli. Mitotic figures are identified, approximately 6 per 10 high-power fields (HPF). Focal areas of dirty necrosis (luminal necrotic debris) are present within the malignant glands, a morphologic feature characteristic of colorectal origin.
- No squamous differentiation identified. No neuroendocrine morphology identified. No mucinous or signet ring cell features identified.
- The surrounding hepatic parenchyma shows mild sinusoidal dilation and mild reactive hepatocyte changes. Portal tracts are mildly expanded. No significant hepatic steatosis. No cirrhosis. Background liver parenchyma appears non-cirrhotic with mildly distorted architecture attributed to compression by tumor.
- Treatment effect: No significant treatment-related fibrosis or hyalinization. Viable tumor is dominant throughout the specimen. The proportion of viable tumor is estimated at greater than 95%; fibrotic/necrotic tissue constitutes less than 5% of the specimen. This is consistent with minimal or no histologic treatment response (Ryan score equivalent: Grade 3 -- no or minimal regression).
- No lymphovascular invasion identified within the biopsy cores (assessment limited by specimen type).

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
ANCILLARY STUDIES
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY (performed in-house, interpreted by attending pathologist):

Cytokeratin 20 (CK20):    POSITIVE -- diffuse, strong cytoplasmic staining in neoplastic cells
CDX2:                     POSITIVE -- diffuse, strong nuclear staining in neoplastic cells
Cytokeratin 7 (CK7):      NEGATIVE -- absent in neoplastic cells; positive internal control (bile duct epithelium) confirmed
TTF-1:                    NEGATIVE -- no nuclear staining in neoplastic cells
Synaptophysin:            NEGATIVE
Chromogranin A:           NEGATIVE
Villin:                   POSITIVE -- brush border-like apical staining pattern in neoplastic glands

Interpretation: The immunophenotype (CK20+, CDX2+, CK7-, TTF-1-) is consistent with metastatic adenocarcinoma of colorectal origin. The immunoprofile is morphologically concordant with the previously established sigmoid colon primary.

MISMATCH REPAIR (MMR) PROTEIN IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY (in-house IHC):

MLH1:   INTACT (preserved nuclear expression in neoplastic cells; positive internal control: stromal lymphocytes and hepatocytes staining appropriately)
MSH2:   INTACT (preserved nuclear expression)
MSH6:   INTACT (preserved nuclear expression)
PMS2:   INTACT (preserved nuclear expression)

Interpretation: Intact MMR protein expression in all four proteins. Result is consistent with MICROSATELLITE STABLE (MSS) tumor. No acquired loss of MMR protein expression identified at time of re-biopsy. This finding is unchanged from original diagnostic biopsy (prior report on file).

KI-67 PROLIFERATIVE INDEX:
  Ki-67 (MIB-1 clone): POSITIVE in approximately 45% of neoplastic nuclei (moderate-to-high proliferative activity).
  Interpretation: Moderate-to-high proliferative index. This value is consistent with moderately differentiated colorectal adenocarcinoma and has not significantly changed compared to prior biopsy.

HER2 (ERBB2) IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY:
  HER2 (4B5 clone, Ventana): EQUIVOCAL -- incomplete and faint lateral/basolateral membrane staining in greater than 10% of tumor cells, scored 2+ per ASCO/CAP colorectal HER2 testing guidelines.
  Note: ISH (in situ hybridization) for HER2 gene amplification is RECOMMENDED and has been ORDERED (PENDING -- see addendum when available).

ADDITIONAL STUDIES -- STATUS PENDING:

  1. HER2 FLUORESCENCE IN SITU HYBRIDIZATION (FISH/CISH): Ordered on block A1 per equivocal IHC result. Result PENDING. To be reported as an addendum to this report.

  2. COMPREHENSIVE GENOMIC PROFILING (CGP), NEXT-GENERATION SEQUENCING: Tumor tissue from block A1 has been submitted to [EXTERNAL LABORATORY -- REDACTED] for 324-gene comprehensive genomic profiling panel, including assessment of KRAS, NRAS, BRAF, HER2, PIK3CA, PTEN, TMB, and MSI status by NGS. Result PENDING. To be reported separately.

  3. CONCURRENT LIQUID BIOPSY (CIRCULATING TUMOR DNA, ctDNA): Plasma sample collected on the same date and submitted separately to [EXTERNAL LABORATORY -- REDACTED] for ctDNA analysis. Result PENDING. To be reported separately.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
FINAL PATHOLOGIC DIAGNOSIS
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

A.  CT-GUIDED CORE NEEDLE BIOPSY, LIVER, SEGMENT VII MASS:
    -- METASTATIC ADENOCARCINOMA, MODERATELY DIFFERENTIATED (GRADE 2)
    -- MORPHOLOGY AND IMMUNOPHENOTYPE CONSISTENT WITH COLORECTAL PRIMARY (CK20+, CDX2+, CK7-, TTF-1-)
    -- MISMATCH REPAIR PROTEINS INTACT BY IHC (MLH1, MSH2, MSH6, PMS2 ALL RETAINED): CONSISTENT WITH MICROSATELLITE STABLE (MSS) TUMOR
    -- NO ACQUIRED MMR DEFICIENCY IDENTIFIED
    -- NO SIGNIFICANT HISTOLOGIC TREATMENT EFFECT (VIABLE TUMOR >95%; RYAN EQUIVALENT GRADE 3)
    -- KI-67 PROLIFERATIVE INDEX: APPROXIMATELY 45%
    -- HER2 IHC: 2+ (EQUIVOCAL); HER2 FISH PENDING (SEE ADDENDUM)
    -- NO SQUAMOUS, NEUROENDOCRINE, OR MUCINOUS DIFFERENTIATION
    -- ADDITIONAL MOLECULAR STUDIES PENDING (SEE ANCILLARY STUDIES SECTION)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
COMMENT
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The histologic and immunophenotypic features of this CT-guided core needle biopsy of the hepatic segment VII lesion are consistent with metastatic colorectal adenocarcinoma, concordant with the previously established sigmoid colon primary (original diagnostic pathology on file). The tumor is moderately differentiated, grade 2, and morphologically similar to the original colonoscopic biopsy specimen.

There is no significant histologic evidence of treatment effect (Ryan equivalent Grade 3), consistent with the clinical scenario of ongoing viable metastatic disease in the liver during systemic chemotherapy. Intact MMR protein expression by IHC (MLH1, MSH2, MSH6, PMS2) confirms MSS status, with no evidence of acquired MMR deficiency -- a potentially important finding as MSI-H acquisition can occur in rare cases following chemotherapy and would have implications for immunotherapy eligibility.

The equivocal (2+) HER2 IHC result in this re-biopsy specimen is a new finding relative to the prior biopsy (which showed HER2 non-amplified status). HER2 FISH has been ordered to clarify HER2 amplification status. Correlation with comprehensive genomic profiling (CGP) results (pending) will be important. Acquired HER2 amplification is a recognized resistance mechanism in KRAS-mutant colorectal cancer, particularly following anti-VEGF-based therapy, and may have potential therapeutic implications if confirmed. Clinical correlation with the treating oncologist is recommended.

No germline implications are identified from this somatic tumor specimen; germline testing was previously completed (result on file).

Results of HER2 FISH, NGS comprehensive genomic profiling, and ctDNA liquid biopsy will be issued as addenda or separate reports as results become available.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
ELECTRONICALLY SIGNED BY: [PATHOLOGIST NAME, MD], Attending Pathologist, Department of Pathology and Laboratory Medicine
DIGITAL SIGNATURE DATE/TIME: [DATE] [TIME]
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*** ADDENDUM (ANTICIPATED -- TO BE ISSUED WHEN HER2 FISH RESULT AVAILABLE) ***

HER2 FISH (FLUORESCENCE IN SITU HYBRIDIZATION) -- RESULT PENDING
Method: Dual-probe FISH using HER2 (17q12) and chromosome 17 centromere (CEP17) probes on formalin-fixed paraffin-embedded tissue, block A1.
Status: Result pending. Report to follow as addendum to this surgical pathology case.

*** END OF REPORT ***

Report generated electronically. All diagnoses are based on light microscopic examination of hematoxylin and eosin (H&E)-stained sections with ancillary immunohistochemical studies as described above. For questions regarding this report, please contact the Department of Pathology.
